jobs.basic_qualifications: - 薬剤師免許の資格を有していること
jobs.description:
Amazonの医薬品専用物流センターにて薬剤師職を募集致します。
（このポジションの勤務地は千葉県千葉市美浜区です。）

Amazonで医薬品販売するために医薬品専用物流センターにおいて、医薬品販売するために必要な、お客様への情報提供、適正販売の判断、薬機法遵守した販売のサポート等をおこなっていただく業務となります。

特に医薬品に関するプロジェクトにおいて、医薬品販売に関わる業務となります。主にウェブサイトを通じてお客様が医薬品を購入する際に、適正販売の判断を中心に担当していただきます。
薬機法遵守の上で適正販売を判断することで、お客様に販売できる商品のセレクションの拡大に貢献頂きます。

このポジションでは、お客様から医薬品に関する電話での問合せ業務もありますので、お客様と円滑にコミュニケーションする必要がございます。電話を通しお客様への正しい情報提供を行うことで、セルフメディケーションを推進してくことが重要となります。

Key job responsibilities
チームとしての職責
・ Customerへの正しい情報提供と適正販売を行う。
・ Amazon.co.jp上の医薬品商品説明に責任を持つ。
・ 医薬品、医療機器等の出荷業務を確認する。
・ 物流センターにおける安全および衛生に関する助言および安全衛生活動を実施する。

Junior Medical Specialistとしての職責
・ 薬機法関連法規の遵守
・ 問い合わせが合ったお客様の症状により、お客様の求めている医薬品の服用可否の判断を行う。
・ 医薬品を適正に使用するための服薬指導、情報提供を実施する。
・ 医薬品の購入者ごとに提供すべき情報の範囲を判断する。
・ 医薬品の購入者から、医薬品副作用の苦情や相談を受け付ける。
・ 一般用医薬品で対応できないと判断した場合、医療機関への受診を勧める。
・ コミュニケーションを通じ、副作用相談など、購入者のアフターケアを実施する。
・ 必要に応じてPMDAへの報告を実施する。

A day in the life
業務内容
60% 医薬品に関するプロジェクトにおいて医薬品適正販売の判断
30% Customerとのコミュニケーション
10% CSチームとのCustomer対応に関するコミュニケーション

勤務形態：シフト勤務になりますが、ご都合に合うシフトを複数ご用意しております。
面接時にご希望シフトを確認させていただきます。
※基本2か月前にシフト作成。勤務日希望、応相談
・1日5時間勤務　週4日　週20時間
・1日10時間勤務　週3日　週30時間
・1日6時間勤務　週4日　週24時間
・1日7時間勤務　週4日　週28時間
・1日8時間勤務　週4日　週32時間
・1日8時間勤務　週3日　週24時間
・1日7時間勤務　週3日　週21時間

雇用形態：契約社員 / 時給 2,400円

About the team
■フルフィルメントセンター部門のご紹介：フルフィルメントセンター (FC)
■職種：その他

✓ Amazon は男女雇用機会均等法を順守しています。 人種、 出身国、性別、性的指向、障がい、年齢、その他の属性によって差別することなく、 平等に採用選考の機会を提供しています。障がいをお持ちの方は、Disability Accommodations
をご覧ください。

- 業務または工程の改善経験（規模の大小は問わず）

jobs.description:
※このポジションの勤務地は岡山県岡山市です。

私たちは数億という膨大な商品を取り扱っており、多くのサプライヤーから入荷したアイテムはフルフィルメントセンターという物流拠点に集約され、各地のデリバリーステーション（配送拠点）を経てお客様先に届けられます。

現在、私たちは物流網を拡大すべくデリバリーステーションの全国展開に注力中。
様々な業界出身（小売・飲食、営業、製造業など）のメンバーが多く活躍しており、メンバー一丸となってミッション達成の為取り組んでいます。
Amazonの重要拠点で物流を進化させる新規ビジネスに携わる。
そんな挑みがいのあるミッションに、あなたの経験を活かしてみませんか。

【業務内容】
作成された日々の計画に沿って、作業工程が計画通りに円滑に進むようサポートを行います。
･社内ツールを使った生産管理業務
･チームメンバーと一緒に現場の課題に対し改善プランの策定、実行
･DSP (運送業務委託先) と協力して配送状況の把握
･安全・品質・作業効率の向上に向けたプロジェクトの立案および推進
･標準作業手順書（SOP）の作成や業務標準化の推進

*マネージャーやチームのサポートのもと上記業務を行っていただきます
*デリバリーステーション内での配送を通して、物流の仕組みやマネージメントを学べます。
*マネージャーへのキャリアアップや社内公募による他部署への異動のチャンスもあります。
*勤務はシフト制となります（夜勤有）

本ポジションの所属部門等に関する詳細は、下記リンク先をご参照ください。

======================
関連情報
・所属部門:ラストマイル(AMZL)
・職種:オペレーション
・勤務地:中国・四国エリアDS
・シフトアシスタント職についてもっと知りたい場合はこちら
======================

オペレーション部門のDEIの取り組み（Japanese Only）

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 5+ years of engineering team management experience
- 10+ years of engineering experience
- 5+ years of Software Engineer, Software Developer, or related occupational experience
- Bachelor's degree or foreign equivalent in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or a related field
- Knowledge of engineering practices and patterns for the full software/hardware/networks development life cycle, including coding standards, code reviews, source control management, build processes, testing, certification, and livesite operations
- Experience partnering with product or program management teams
- Experience partnering with product and program management teams
- Experience managing multiple concurrent programs, projects and development teams in both Agile and waterfall environments
jobs.description:
We are looking for a talented and enthusiastic Software Development Manager who has strong customer obsession and a track record of delivering innovative products and features. You will lead a globe-spanning team of engineers, technical program managers, and accessibility testers, along with their managers as they develop new accessibility features for our devices and Alexa, as well as to create AI-powered tooling for accessibility testing, and to improve developer efficiency to help them deliver accessible offerings for our customers. Your team will work at the intersection accessibility and AI, for both our mature Fire OS operating system, for our recently introduced Vega OS (whose accessibility framework we built from scratch), as well as for Alexa.

This role requires a significant autonomy, drive, and the ability to problem solve. You will be well suited to this role if you are customer-focused, resourceful, team-oriented, and have an ability to work independently under time constraints. You will work and influence internal and external stakeholders, manage cross functional programs, and be able to help prioritize amidst ambitious goals and timelines.


Key job responsibilities
- You will be responsible for leading a strong team of engineers, both directly and through their engineering managers, to innovate and deliver accessibility solutions for our customers with disabilities
- You will leverage AI to build developer and QA tools to increase developer & testing velocity, as well as in customer-facing features
- You will work backwards from the customer to invent new solutions and to improve existing software
- You will work with the executive leadership and technical leaders to define the product direction; create vision and execution documents; and insist on the high bar for technical documentation and the quality of the developed software
- You may periodically perform hands-on code reviews and other technical deep dives to ensure the right thing is being built and delivered to customers
- You will hire, coach, and promote your staff

A day in the life
In this role, no two days are the same — and that’s by design. You will spend a good part of your time engaging with a wide range of partner teams across product, UX, research, engineering, legal, and other product teams to gather insights, clarify accessibility requirements, and uncover both issues and opportunities.

You’ll participate in regular brainstorming sessions to shape new ideas, solve complex accessibility problems, and design fixes that address not just immediate needs, but also set us up for long-term success.

You will meet with customers at disability conferences throughout the year, representing Amazon and our accessibility offerings, and learning about what customers love, what could work better, and what they wish we might offer.

About the team
You would be joining Amazon’s longest tenured, accessibility team. Over the past 13 years, their work was recognized by American Foundation for the Blind with their Helen Keller Achievement award, by Disability:IN with their Marketplace Innovator award, and by FCC Chairman Ajit Pai with the FCC Chairman’s Award for Advancements in Accessibility (among others). Your colleagues bring over a century of combined experience in the field, and include Amazon’s sole MacArthur Fellow, and two of the only three people to be recognized twice with Helen Keller Achievement Awards for their work.

jobs.description:
The Global Data Center Availability Team at Amazon Web Services is looking for a Computerized Maintenance Management Systems (CMMS) Manager to lead medium to large scale availability programs within the team.

The CMMS Manager will be called upon to assist in data center evaluations, optimization, and improvements of data center environments globally, which maintain the highest performance standards and utilize innovation to reduce cost and increase efficiency.

The candidate must have operational data center experience and experience managing CMMS schedules to track power and cooling assets, schedule maintenance, and track spare parts.

The manager will support multiple internal business organizations and will be responsible for communication of program statuses and metrics. The manager must be able to communicate any issues and/or risk in a clear and professional manner to ensure appropriate actions are met by internal Amazon stakeholders.

Key job responsibilities
As a CMMS Manager, you will:

Manage a team of 8-12 employees, developing their technical and leadership capabilities through coaching, performance management, and career development planning

Serve as a key business partner working directly with global operations teams, inter-departmental business partners and internal stakeholders

Interact with engineers, technicians, project managers, and Sr. leaders to develop your programs

Analyze complex, ambiguous, and potentially escalated business issues to develop recommendations for the operations leadership team

Drive process change and improvement across multiple business and program teams, including strategic initiatives to reduce asset collection time, improve asset data accuracy, and enhance available asset attributes to meet evolving customer needs**

Report ad hoc, weekly, and monthly program and operating metrics

Run meetings, create and maintain timelines, and keep large, diverse groups informed of progress and obstacles

Demonstrate excellent interpersonal and communication skills (both verbal and written) and proven ability to work effectively with all organizational levels

Define and execute on team goals and operational strategies that align with broader CMMS and Availability Team objectives

A day in the life
A typical day for this individual would be a mix of meeting (in person/remote) and influencing stakeholders across multiple businesses and global regions. Non-meeting time will be focused developing project plans, aligning on milestones across teams, doc writing, developing/monitoring metrics, understand end customer requirements and pain points, and reporting on status of efforts. You will be responsible for the day to day management and growth of your team.

About the team
The Availability team is responsible for developing power and cooling maintenance programs. The team defines scopes of work for these asset types, defines spares standards, develops tooling to gather PM data and identify failures, develops digital procedures to execute work, and manages CMMS tooling to track assets, work, labor, and spares. The team acts as the global process owners for Engineering Operations Technicians operating critical DC infrastructure to support power and cooling to servers.

jobs.description:
Do you want to be a leader in the team that takes last mile delivery in the Transportation models to the next generation? Do you have a solid analytical thinking and want to solve problems with solutions that will meet the growing worldwide need? If you have passion to learn and be curious, and want apply your programming and analytical skills to help Amazon grow, then Last Mile Delivery Service Partner (Last Mile DSP) is the team for you. We are looking for top notch SDEs to be part of our world class software development team.

DSP (Delivery Service Partners) are medium and large sized 3rd party businesses delivering packages from amazon last mile delivery stations to the customer on behalf of Amazon. The team’s global mission is to become Amazon’s logistics labor capacity of choice by building the provider network required to support Amazon’s growth and meet customer expectations for any deliveries, at any time of the year through scalable, innovative, and low cost operations, and in a way that both delights customers and empowers DSPs and their Delivery Associates.

The DSP Engagement tech owns and develops technical infrastructure for a multi-tenant omni channel platform to create and send communications across Amazon Delivery partners, delivering 100M individual notifications per year. We own the how, the when and the what of all communications sent to Amazon delivery partners.

SDE2s in this team will be independent problem solvers creating business impact through their tech deliverables. They will learn, grow and create a lasting impact to business and their careers while having fun at work.

Key job responsibilities
• You can effectively articulate technical challenges and solutions to tech and non-tech audiences.
• You have experience with architecting systems involving data processing components, workflow orchestration and dependency management.
• You have expertise in the creation and management of backend and frontend applications and data stores, as well as proven ability to translate data into meaningful insights.
• You use object-oriented design concepts to program large multi-tier systems.

A day in the life
You will be part of a team that is responsible for all aspects of logistics capacity, onboarding and customer experience areas covering visual interface to the back end services. You will be working with technologies to improve usability and increase resiliency and scalability.

You will translate business requirements to technical deliverables and deliver operationally stable solution that provide good customer experience.

Candidates need to have strong ownership, passion to provide great customer experience and excellent troubleshooting skills. Experience building resilient web/mobile loved applications and/or web services-based applications is desired.

We strongly believe in work life harmony, diversity, recognition, career and fun@work

jobs.description:
Join Amazon’s growing RME (Reliability, Maintenance & Engineering) team as a Maintenance Supervisor at our Craigieburn site, leading a team of 2 Technicians (fitters and electricians).

Our Reliability Maintenance Engineering (RME) team is central to Amazon's commitment to innovation. As Amazon evolves and adapts, this team makes sure that the tools and technologies we use do as well. As a Maintenance Supervisor you'll help us stay one step ahead, adopting the latest technologies and identifying new and efficient ways of working. In this role, you’ll pay close attention to our processes to help maintain our high standards, and you’ll put in place upgrades to take that standard even higher.

Learn more about Amazon RME in Australia

Key job responsibilities
- Lead and Motivate: Supervise a team of maintenance technicians, providing strategic guidance and professional development
- Develop and implement comprehensive preventative maintenance programs and facility compliance strategies
- Act as an ambassador for safety within the team and to promote safe working across the site
- Install, maintain, and troubleshoot complex facility systems and automated equipment
- Cross-Functional Collaboration: Build strong relationships across organizational levels and participate in strategic leadership meetings

A day in the life
Your day will be a dynamic blend of hands-on technical work and strategic leadership. You'll lead a team of technicians (fitters and electricians), troubleshoot complex equipment challenges, and develop proactive maintenance strategies that keep our cutting-edge systems performing at their peak.

jobs.title: Maintenance Supervisor

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Formal trade qualification: Cert III In Electrotechnology holding an unrestricted trade licence OR Cert III Mechanical Engineering (Fitter/Turner). Alternatively, equivalent trade qualifications formally recognised in Australia.
- Industrial maintenance experience.
- Leadership experience in supervising multi-trade teams in a fast paced industrial manufacturing/distribution center environment.
- Experience in mentoring and developing technicians.
- Relevant experience in automation or material handling equipment environments.
- Experience using CMMS (Computerized Maintenance Management Systems) to plan proactive maintenance and record reactive tasks.
- Experience in contractor management.
jobs.description:
Join Amazon’s growing Reliability, Maintenance & Engineering (RME) team as a Maintenance Supervisor at the largest Fulfillment Center (FC) in the Southern Hemisphere and first Amazon Robotics Site in Australia.

Our RME team is central to Amazon's commitment to innovation. As Amazon evolves and adapts, this team makes sure that the tools and technologies we use do as well. As a Maintenance Supervisor you'll help us stay one step ahead, adopting the latest technologies and identifying new and efficient ways of working. In this role, you’ll pay close attention to our processes to help maintain our high standards, and you’ll put in place upgrades to take that standard even higher.

The Maintenance Supervisor is the shift lead for a team of up to 10 Technicians (fitters and electricians). During each shift, you'll lead the team in executing preventative maintenance to maximize equipment uptime. You'll maintain a strong focus on safety by monitoring processes and procedures to ensure compliance with all regulations and policies.

Learn more about Amazon RME in Australia

Key job responsibilities
- Lead and Motivate: Supervise a team of maintenance technicians, providing strategic guidance and professional development
- Develop and implement comprehensive preventative maintenance programs and facility compliance strategies
- Act as an ambassador for safety within the team and to promote safe working across the site
- Install, maintain, and troubleshoot complex facility systems and automated equipment
- Cross-Functional Collaboration: Build strong relationships across organizational levels and participate in strategic leadership meetings

A day in the life
Your day will be a dynamic blend of hands-on technical work and strategic leadership. You'll lead a team of technicians (fitters and electricians), troubleshoot complex equipment challenges, and develop proactive maintenance strategies that keep our cutting-edge systems performing at their peak.
